issues.description: ban/kick enforcement only leaves the main space room. If the socket previously joined a party room, it remains subscribed to `party-${spaceId}-${partyId}` until the delayed disconnect fires, so the kicked/banned user can still receive party messages or party updates during the 250ms grace window. This means access is not fully revoked synchronously.
issues.recommendation: During ban/kick, synchronously leave all application rooms for each target socket, or at least the matching party room, and clear `socket.data.partyId` before starting the disconnect grace timer.

issues.description: `leave:space` trusts the client-supplied `spaceId` and `leaveSpace` does not clear `socket.data.spaceId`, role, restriction, or party state. After a normal leave, the socket is no longer in the room but still passes all handlers that authorize using `socket.data.spaceId`, allowing stale chat/admin/editor/media operations against the old space. During enforcement grace, the same unauthenticated leave path can also be re-emitted with the old spaceId and produce duplicate `player:left`.
issues.recommendation: Make `leave:space` use and validate the current `socket.data.spaceId`, return if it does not match, and have `leaveSpace` clear all space-scoped socket data and sub-room membership after removing presence.
